---
title: "References — Tidyverse"
---

The full [tidyverse](https://www.tidyverse.org/) framework — the meta-package, its core members, and the surrounding `r-lib` toolchain that most modern R analyses lean on.

## Meta and design

- Wickham et al. *Welcome to the tidyverse*. JOSS, 2019. The framing paper for the family.
- Wickham, Çetinkaya-Rundel, Grolemund. *R for Data Science* (2nd ed.). O'Reilly, 2023. The canonical entry-point text.

## Core packages

- [**dplyr**](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/) — grammar of data manipulation.
- [**tidyr**](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/) — tidy-data shape transformations.
- [**readr**](https://readr.tidyverse.org/) — fast, friendly reading of rectangular data.
- [**tibble**](https://tibble.tidyverse.org/) — modern reimagining of the data frame.
- [**purrr**](https://purrr.tidyverse.org/) — functional programming with consistent type contracts.
- [**stringr**](https://stringr.tidyverse.org/) — string manipulation, ICU-backed.
- [**forcats**](https://forcats.tidyverse.org/) — tools for working with factors.
- [**ggplot2**](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/) — grammar of graphics.
- [**lubridate**](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/) — date and date-time arithmetic.

## Foundational papers

- Wickham. *Tidy Data*. *Journal of Statistical Software*, 2014. The conceptual basis for `tidyr` and the tidyverse data shape.
- Wickham. *A Layered Grammar of Graphics*. *Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics*, 2010. The basis for `ggplot2`.

## Adjacent r-lib toolchain

These are not part of the tidyverse meta-package but share its API conventions and maintainers:

- [**rlang**](https://rlang.r-lib.org/) — tidy-eval foundations.
- [**vctrs**](https://vctrs.r-lib.org/) — typed vector primitives shared across the tidyverse.
- [**glue**](https://glue.tidyverse.org/) — string interpolation.
- [**fs**](https://fs.r-lib.org/) — cross-platform filesystem operations.
- [**httr2**](https://httr2.r-lib.org/) — modern HTTP client.

See the [disclaimer](index.qmd#disclaimer) — this is a starting list, not a curated bibliography.
